Paragraph.Text: a trust or estate in which the offeror has a substantial beneficial interest or in respect of which they serve as a trustee or a liquidator of the succession or in a similar capacity,

Subsection.Text: The definition take-over bid in subsection 283(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: offre d’achat visant à la mainmise

Definition.Text: take-over bid means an offer made by an offeror at approximately the same time to all of the shareholders of a distributing bank to acquire all of the shares of a class of issued shares, and includes an offer by a distributing bank to repurchase all of the shares of a class.

Subsection.Text: The definitions banque pollicitée, pollicitant and pollicité in subsection 283(1) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: banque pollicitée Banque dont les actions font l’objet d’une offre d’achat visant à la mainmise.
pollicitant Toute personne, à l’exception du mandataire, qui fait une offre d’achat visant à la mainmise et, en outre, les personnes qui conjointement ou de concert, même indirectement :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: soit font une telle offre;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: soit ont l’intention d’exercer les droits de vote dont sont assorties les actions faisant l’objet d’une telle offre.

Definition.Text: pollicité Toute personne à laquelle est faite une offre dans le cadre d’une offre d’achat visant à la mainmise.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 283(1) of the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: offre d’achat visant à la mainmise

Definition.Text: offre d’achat visant à la mainmise L’offre qu’un pollicitant adresse à peu près au même moment à tous les actionnaires d’une banque ayant fait appel au public pour acquérir toutes les actions d’une catégorie d’actions émises. Y est assimilée la pollicitation d’une telle banque visant le rachat de toutes les actions d’une catégorie de ses actions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 283(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
L’offre d’achat visant à la mainmise est réputée être datée du jour de son envoi.

Section.Text: Section 284 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Le pollicitant peut, en se conformant aux articles 285 à 290, aux paragraphes 291(1) et (2) et à l’article 292, acquérir les actions des pollicités opposants lorsque l’offre d’achat visant à la mainmise est, dans les cent vingt jours suivant la date où elle est faite, acceptée par les détenteurs d’au moins quatre-vingt-dix pour cent des actions de la catégorie en cause, sans qu’il soit tenu compte des actions détenues à la date de l’offre d’achat visant à la mainmise, même indirectement, par lui-même, les personnes morales de son groupe ou ses associés.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 285(1) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Le pollicitant peut acquérir les actions des pollicités opposants en leur faisant parvenir ainsi qu’au surintendant, par courrier recommandé, dans les soixante jours suivant la date d’expiration de l’offre d’achat visant à la mainmise et, en tout état de cause, dans les cent quatre-vingts jours suivant la date de l’offre d’achat visant à la mainmise, un avis précisant à la fois :
Paragraph 285(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a dissenting offeree who does not notify the offeror in accordance with paragraph 286(b) is deemed to have elected to transfer the shares to the offeror on the same terms on which the offeror acquired the shares from the offerees who accepted the take-over bid; and

Section.Text: Section 286 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A dissenting offeree to whom a notice is sent under subsection 285(1) shall within 20 days after receiving the notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: send to the offeree bank the share certificates representing the shares to which the take-over bid relates; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: elect to transfer the shares to the offeror on the same terms as those on which the offeror acquired shares from the offerees who accepted the take-over bid or to demand payment of the fair value of the shares in accordance with sections 289 to 292 by notifying the offeror.

Section.Text: A dissenting offeree who does not notify the offeror in accordance with paragraph 286(b) is deemed to have elected to transfer the shares to the offeror on the same terms as those on which the offeror acquired shares from the offerees who accepted the take-over bid.
Subsection 287(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Within 20 days after the offeror sends a notice under subsection 285(1), the offeror shall pay the money, or transfer the other consideration, to the offeree bank that the offeror would have had to pay or transfer to a dissenting offeree if the dissenting offeree had elected to transfer their shares in accordance with paragraph 286(b).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 287:

A bank that is making a take-over bid to repurchase all of the shares of a class is deemed to hold in a fiduciary capacity for the dissenting shareholders the money that it would have had to pay, and the other consideration that it would have had to transfer, to a dissenting offeree if the dissenting offeree had elected to transfer their shares in accordance with paragraph 286(b). The bank shall within 20 days after a notice is sent under subsection 285(1) deposit the money in a separate account in another deposit-taking financial institution in Canada and place any other consideration in the custody of another deposit-taking financial institution in Canada.
Paragraphs 288(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the payment or transfer required by subsection 287(1) is made, issue to the offeror a share certificate in respect of the shares that were held by the dissenting offerees;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: give to each dissenting offeree who elects to transfer shares under paragraph 286(b) and who sends the share certificates as required under paragraph 286(a) the money or other consideration to which they are entitled, disregarding fractional shares, which may be paid for in money; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the payment or transfer required by subsection 287(1) is made and the money or other consideration is deposited as required by subsections 287(2) and (3) or by section 287.1, send to each dissenting offeree who has not sent share certificates as required under paragraph 286(a) a notice stating that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: their shares have been cancelled,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the offeree bank or its designated person holds in a fiduciary capacity for that offeree the money or other consideration to which they are entitled as payment for or in exchange for the shares, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the offeree bank will, subject to sections 289 to 292, send that money or other consideration to that offeree without delay after receiving the share certificates.

Section.Text: Subsection 289(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a dissenting offeree has elected to demand payment of the fair value of their shares under paragraph 286(b), the offeror may, within 20 days after it has paid the money or transferred the other consideration under subsection 287(1), apply to a court to fix the fair value of the shares of that dissenting offeree.

Section.Text: Paragraph 290(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all dissenting offerees who have made elections to demand payment under paragraph 286(b) and whose shares have not been acquired by the offeror shall be joined as parties and are bound by the decision of the court; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 291(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fix the amount of money or other consideration that is deemed to be held in a fiduciary capacity under subsection 287(2) or section 287.1;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 292:

Subsection.Text: If a shareholder who holds shares of an offeree bank does not receive the notice referred to in subsection 285(1), the shareholder may require the offeror to acquire the shares

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within 90 days after the date of termination of the take-over bid; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the shareholder did not receive an offer under the take-over bid, within 90 days after the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date of termination of the take-over bid, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the shareholder learned of the take-over bid.

Subsection.Text: If the shareholder requires the offeror to acquire shares, the offeror shall acquire them on the same terms as those on which the offeror acquires shares from offerees who accept the take-over bid.

Section.Text: Subsection 308(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: With respect to each of the financial years to which it relates, the annual statement of a bank must contain the prescribed statements and any information that is in the opinion of the directors necessary to present fairly, in accord­ance with the accounting principles referred to in subsection (4), the financial position of the bank as at the end of the financial year to which it relates and the results of the operations and changes in the financial position of the bank for that financial year.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 309(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors of a bank shall approve the annual statement and their approval shall be evidenced by the signature or a printed or otherwise mechanically reproduced facsimile of the signature of

Section.Text: Subsection 311(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank shall, no later than 21 days before the date of each annual meeting or before the signing of a resolution under paragraph 152(1)(b) in lieu of the annual meeting, send to each shareholder a copy of the documents referred to in subsections 308(1) and (3) unless that time period is waived by the shareholder.
The portion of paragraph 315(2)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member of a firm of accountants is deemed not to be independent of a bank if that member, a business partner of that member or the firm of accountants

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a business partner, director, officer or employee of the bank or of any affiliate of the bank or is a business partner of any director, officer or employee of the bank or of any affiliate of the bank,

Subsection.Text: Section 315 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsection (2), a business partner of a member of a firm of accountants includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: another member of the firm; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a shareholder of the firm or of a business partner of the member.

Section.Text: Subsection 321(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In the case of a proposed replacement of an auditor whether because of removal or the expiry of their term, the bank shall make a statement of the reasons for the proposed replacement and the proposed replacement auditor may make a statement in which they comment on those reasons.

The bank shall send a copy of the statements referred to in subsections (1) and (1.1) without delay to every shareholder entitled to vote at the annual meeting of shareholders and to the Superintendent.

Section.Text: Paragraph 328(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor or auditors shall, at the time of transmitting the report to the chief executive officer and chief financial officer, provide the audit committee of the bank and the Superintendent with a copy.

Section.Text: Paragraph 334(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the complainant has, not less than 14 days before bringing the application or as otherwise ordered by the court, given notice to the directors of the bank or the bank’s subsidiary of the complainant’s intention to apply to the court under subsection (1) if the directors of the bank or the bank’s subsidiary do not bring, diligently prosecute or defend or discontinue the action;

Section.Text: Subsection 337(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Les plaignants ne sont pas tenus de fournir de cautionnement pour les frais.

Section.Text: Paragraph 349(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: nommer un liquidateur en exigeant ou non un cautionnement, fixer sa rémunération et le remplacer;

Section.Text: Section 354 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A liquidator is not liable if they exercised the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would have exercised in comparable circumstances, including reliance in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the bank represented to the liquidator by an officer of the bank or in a written report of the auditor or auditors of the bank fairly to reflect the financial condition of the bank; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by them.

Section.Text: Paragraph 527(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the province in which the principal office of the authorized foreign bank is to be situated;

Section.Text: Paragraph 528(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: change the name under which it is permitted to carry on business in Canada or the province in which its principal office is to be situated as that name or province is set out in the order made under subsection 524(1) or in any other order made under this section;

Section.Text: Subsections 535(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An authorized foreign bank shall at all times have a principal office in the province specified in the order made under subsection 524(1) or 528(1) with respect to it.
An authorized foreign bank may change the address of its principal office within the province specified in the order made under subsection 524(1) or 528(1) with respect to it.
The portion of paragraph 585(3)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member of a firm of accountants is deemed not to be independent of an authorized foreign bank if that member, a business partner of that member or the firm of accountants

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a business partner, director, officer or employee of the authorized foreign bank or of any affiliate of the authorized foreign bank or is a business partner of any director, officer or employee of the authorized foreign bank or of any affiliate of the authorized foreign bank,

Subsection.Text: Section 585 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
For the purposes of subsection (3), a business partner of a member of a firm of accountants includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: another member of the firm; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a shareholder of the firm or of another business partner of the member.

Section.Text: Section 669 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Authority of directors, officers and representatives

Subsection.Text: No bank holding company and no guarantor of an obligation of a bank holding company may assert against a person dealing with the bank holding company or against a person who has acquired rights from the bank holding company that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the bank holding company’s incorporating instrument or any by-laws of the bank holding company have not been complied with;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons named as directors of the bank holding company in the most recent return sent to the Superintendent under section 951 are not the directors of the bank holding company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the place named in the incorporating instrument or by-laws of the bank holding company is not the place where the head office of the bank holding company is situated;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person held out by the bank holding company as a director, officer or representative of the bank holding company has not been duly appointed or has no authority to exercise the powers and perform the duties that are customary in the business of the bank holding company or usual for a director, officer or representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a document issued by any director, officer or representative of the bank holding company with actual or usual authority to issue the document is not valid or not genuine.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a person who has or ought to have knowledge of a situation described in that subsection by virtue of their relationship to the bank holding company.

Section.Text: Paragraph 676(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the province in which the head office of the bank holding company is to be situated; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 699(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the province in which the head office of the bank holding company is situated.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 706(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The by-laws of a bank holding company may, subject to any limitations set out in them, authorize the issue of any class of shares in one or more series and may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fix the maximum number of shares in each series and determine the designation, rights, privileges, restrictions and conditions attaching to them; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the directors to do anything referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 706(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the directors exercise their authority under paragraph (1)(b), the directors shall, before the issue of shares of the series, send to the Superintendent a copy of the by-law authorizing the directors to fix the rights, privileges, restrictions and conditions of those shares and shall provide the Superintendent with particulars of the proposed series of shares.

Section.Text: Subsection 710(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), a bank holding company may, subject to subsection (4), record in the stated capital account maintained for the shares of a class or series any part of the consideration it receives in an exchange if it issues shares

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in exchange for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who immediately before the exchange did not deal with the bank holding company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of or another interest in a body corporate that immediately before the exchange or because of it did not deal with the bank holding company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who immediately before the exchange dealt with the bank holding company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act if the person, the bank holding company and all of the holders of shares in the class or series of shares so issued consent to the exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement referred to in subsection 804(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to shareholders of an amalgamating body corporate who receive the shares in addition to or instead of securities of the amalgamated bank holding company.

Section.Text: Subsection 716(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La société de portefeuille bancaire peut autoriser ses filiales à détenir, en qualité de représentant personnel, mais à condition de ne pas en avoir la propriété effective, soit des actions de la société ou d’une personne morale qui la contrôle, soit des titres de participation d’une entité non constituée en personne morale qui la contrôle.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 716:

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company may permit its subsidiary to acquire shares of the bank holding company, shares of an entity that controls the bank holding company or any ownership interests of any unincorporated entity that controls the bank holding company if before the subsidiary acquires them the conditions prescribed for the purposes of this subsection are met.
After a subsidiary has acquired shares or ownership interests in accordance with subsection (1), the conditions prescribed for the purposes of this subsection are to be met.
Despite section 665 and subsection 710(2), the issue and acquisition of the shares or ownership interests are subject to the prescribed requirements if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the bank holding company permits the subsidiary to acquire the shares or ownership interests; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a condition prescribed for the purposes of subsection (1) was not met, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a condition prescribed for the purposes of subsection (2) was not met or ceased to be met.

Section.Text: Paragraph 724(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “subsections 137(5) to (7) and sections 138 to 141 and 145” in subsection 93(1) is to be read as a reference to “subsections 726(5) to (7) and sections 727 to 730 and 734”; and

Section.Text: Section 725 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 725(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any person who is entitled to attend a meeting of shareholders may participate in the meeting by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting if the bank holding company makes one available. A person who is participating in a meeting by one of those means is deemed for the purposes of this Part to be present at the meeting.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the manner of and conditions for participating in a meeting by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting.

Section.Text: Subsections 726(2) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Order to delay calling annual meeting
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the bank holding company may apply to the court for an order extending the time for calling an annual meeting.

The bank holding company shall give notice of the application to the Superintendent before any hearing concerning the application and shall provide the Superintendent with a copy of any order that is issued.
The Superintendent is entitled to appear and be heard in person or by counsel at any hearing concerning the application.

The directors may in advance fix a record date, that is within the prescribed period, for the determination of shareholders for any purpose, including for a determination of which shareholders are entitled to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: receive payment of a dividend;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: participate in a liquidation distribution;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: receive notice of a meeting of shareholders; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: vote at a meeting of shareholders.

Subsection.Text: If no record date is fixed,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of shareholders who are entitled to receive notice of a meeting of shareholders is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the close of business on the day immediately preceding the day on which the notice is given, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice is given, the day on which the meeting is held; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of shareholders for any other purpose, other than to establish a shareholder’s right to vote, is at the close of business on the day on which the directors pass a resolution in respect of that purpose.

Subsection.Text: If a record date is fixed and unless notice of the record date is waived in writing by every holder of a share of the class or series affected whose name is set out in the securities register at the close of business on the day on which the directors fix the record date, notice of the record date shall be given within the prescribed period by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: advertisement in a newspaper in general circulation in the place where the bank holding company’s head office is situated and in each place in Canada where the bank holding company has a transfer agent or where a transfer of its shares may be recorded; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: written notice to each stock exchange in Canada on which the bank holding company’s shares are listed for trading.

Section.Text: Subsection 727(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Notice of the time and place of a meeting of shareholders of a bank holding company shall be sent within the prescribed period to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each shareholder entitled to vote at the meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each director;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor of the bank holding company; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent.

Subsection.Text: In the case of a bank holding company that is not a distributing bank holding company, notice may be sent within any shorter period specified in its by-laws.

Section.Text: Subsection 728(1) of the Act is replaced by the following;

Subsection.Text: A notice of a meeting is not required to be sent to shareholders who are not registered on the records of the bank holding company or the bank holding company’s transfer agent on the record date fixed under paragraph 726(5)(c) or determined under paragraph 726(6)(a).

Section.Text: Subsections 732(1) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (1.1) and (1.2), a registered holder or beneficial owner of shares that may be voted at an annual meeting of shareholders may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: submit to the bank holding company notice of any matter that they propose to raise at the meeting (in this section and section 733 referred to as a “proposal”); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: discuss at the meeting any matter in respect of which they would have been entitled to submit a proposal.

Subsection.Text: To be eligible to submit a proposal a person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for at least the prescribed period be the registered holder or beneficial owner of at least the prescribed number of the bank holding company’s outstanding shares; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have the support of persons who, in the aggregate and including or not including the person who submits the proposal, have for at least the prescribed period been the registered holders or beneficial owners of at least the prescribed number of the bank holding company’s outstanding shares.

Subsection.Text: A proposal is to be accompanied by the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of the person submitting the proposal and the names and addresses of their supporters, if any; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of shares held or owned by the person and their supporters, if any, and the date that the shares were acquired.

Subsection.Text: The information provided under subsection (1.2) does not form part of a proposal or of the supporting statement referred to in subsection (3) and is not to be included for the purpose of the prescribed maximum number of words referred to in subsection (3).

If the bank holding company requests within the prescribed period that a person provide proof that they are eligible to submit a proposal, the person shall within the prescribed period provide proof that they meet the requirements of subsection (1.1).

A bank holding company that solicits proxies shall set out in the management proxy circular required by subsection 156.05(1) or attach to it any proposal submitted for consideration at a meeting of shareholders.

At the request of the person who submits a proposal, the bank holding company shall set out in the management proxy circular or attach to it the person’s statement in support of the proposal and their name and address. The statement and proposal together are not to exceed the prescribed maximum number of words.

A proposal may include nominations for the election of directors if it is signed by one or more registered holders or beneficial owners of shares representing in the aggregate not less than 5% of the shares of the bank holding company or 5% of the shares of a class of its shares entitled to vote at the meeting at which the proposal is to be presented.
A bank holding company is not required to comply with subsections (2) and (3) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proposal is not submitted to the bank holding company at least the prescribed number of days before the anniversary date of the notice of meeting that was sent to shareholders in respect of the previous annual meeting of shareholders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it clearly appears that the primary purpose of the proposal is to enforce a personal claim or redress a personal griev­ance against the bank holding company or its directors, officers or security holders;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: it clearly appears that the proposal does not relate in a significant way to the business or affairs of the bank holding company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person submitting the proposal failed within the prescribed period before the bank holding company receives their proposal to present, in person or by proxy, at a meeting of shareholders a proposal that at their request had been set out in or attached to a management proxy circular;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: substantially the same proposal was set out in or attached to a management proxy circular or dissident’s proxy circular relating to, and presented to shareholders at, a meeting of shareholders held within the prescribed period before the receipt of the proposal and did not receive the prescribed minimum amount of support at the meeting; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the rights conferred by subsections (1) to (4) are being abused to secure publicity.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Bank holding company may refuse to include proposal
Subsection.Text: If a person who submits a proposal fails to continue to hold or own shares in accordance with paragraph (1.1)(a) or, as the case may be, does not continue to have the support of persons who are in the aggregate the registered holders or beneficial owners of the prescribed number of shares in accordance with paragraph (1.1)(b) until the end of the meeting, the bank holding company is not required to set out any proposal submitted by that person in or attach it to a management proxy circular for any meeting held within the prescribed period after the day of the meeting.
Subsections 733(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a bank holding company refuses to include a proposal in a management proxy circular, it shall in writing notify the person submitting the proposal of its intention to omit the proposal from the management proxy circular and of the reasons for the refusal. It shall notify the person within the prescribed period after either the day on which it receives the proposal or, if it has requested proof under subsection 732(1.4), the day on which it receives the proof.

On the application of a person submitting a proposal who claims to be aggrieved by a bank holding company’s refusal under subsection (1), a court may restrain the holding of the meeting at which the proposal is sought to be presented and make any further order that it thinks fit.
Subsection 733(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

La société ou toute personne qui prétend qu’une proposition lui cause un préjudice peut demander au tribunal une ordonnance autorisant la société à ne pas la faire figurer à la circulaire de la direction sollicitant des procurations ou en annexe; le tribunal, s’il est convaincu que le paragraphe 732(5) s’applique, peut rendre en l’espèce la décision qu’il estime pertinente.
Subsections 734(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: List of shareholders entitled to notice

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company shall prepare an alphabetical list of shareholders entitled to receive notice of a meeting showing the number of shares held by each shareholder

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a record date is fixed under paragraph 726(5)(c), no later than 10 days after that date; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if no record date is fixed, on the record date determined under paragraph 726(6)(a).

Subsection.Text: The bank holding company shall prepare an alphabetical list of shareholders entitled to vote as of the record date showing the number of shares held by each shareholder

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a record date is fixed under paragraph 726(5)(d), no later than 10 days after that date; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if no record date is fixed under paragraph 726(5)(d), no later than 10 days after a record date is fixed under paragraph 726(5)(c) or no later than the record date determined under paragraph 726(6)(a), as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 156.09, a shareholder whose name appears on a list prepared under subsection (2) is entitled to vote the shares shown opposite their name.
The portion of subsection 734(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A shareholder may examine the list of shareholders

Section.Text: Section 740 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1) and unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any vote referred to in that subsection may be held entirely by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility if the bank holding company makes one available.

Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any person who is participating in a meeting of shareholders under subsection 725(2) and entitled to vote at that meeting may vote by means of the telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that the bank holding company has made available for that purpose.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the manner of and conditions for voting at a meeting of shareholders by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility.

Section.Text: Section 741 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting that the chairperson declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is in the absence of evidence to the contrary proof of that fact without proof of the number or proportion of votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.

Section.Text: Paragraph 742(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a record date has been fixed under paragraph 726(5)(c) and notice of it has been given under subsection 726(7);

Section.Text: Subsection 743(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Court may order meeting to be called

Subsection.Text: A court may, on the application of a director, a shareholder who is entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders or the Superintendent, order a meeting to be called, held or conducted in the manner that the court directs if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is impracticable to call the meeting within the time or in the manner in which it is to be called;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is impracticable to conduct the meeting in the manner required by this Part or the by-laws; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the court thinks that the meeting ought to be called, held or conducted within the time or in the manner that it directs for any other reason.

Section.Text: Section 746 of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “paragraph 143(1.1)(b)” in subparagraph (b)(vi) of the definition solicitation in section 156.01 is to be read as a reference to “paragraph 732(1.1)(b)”.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 753:
The election or appointment of a person as a director is subject to the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person was present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place and did not refuse to hold office as a director; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person was not present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place but

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: consented in writing to hold office as a director before the election or appointment or within 10 days after it, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: acted as a director after the election or appointment.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 756(1)(g) and (h) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a director may be removed from office only if the number of votes cast in favour of a motion to remove the director is greater than the product of the number of directors required by the by-laws and the number of votes cast against the motion; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the number of directors required by the by-laws may be decreased only if the number of votes cast in favour of a motion to decrease the number of directors is greater than the product of the number of directors required by the by-laws and the number of votes cast against the motion.

Section.Text: Subsection 765(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 772 but subject to subsection (2) and sections 764 and 766, a quorum of directors may fill a vacancy among the directors except a vacancy resulting from a change in the by-laws by which the number or the minimum or maximum number of directors is increased or from a failure to elect the number or minimum number of directors provided for in the by-laws.

Section.Text: Paragraph 766(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the remaining directors elected by the holders of that class or series of shares may fill the vacancy except one resulting from an increase in the number or the minimum or maximum number of directors for that class or series or from a failure to elect the number or minimum number of directors provided for in the by-laws for that class or series;

Section.Text: Subsection 771(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director who is present at a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors but is not, in accordance with subsection 790(1), present at any particular time during the meeting is considered to be present for the purposes of this section.

Section.Text: Section 774 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting that the chairperson declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is in the absence of evidence to the contrary proof of that fact without proof of the number or proportion of votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 785(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fill a vacancy among the directors, on a committee of directors or in the office of auditor or appoint additional directors;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: issue or cause to be issued securities, including an issue of shares of a series that is authorized in accordance with section 706, except in accordance with any authorization made by the directors;

Section.Text: Section 789 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director or officer of a bank holding company shall disclose to the bank holding company, in writing or by requesting to have it entered in the minutes of a meeting of directors or a meeting of a committee of directors, the nature and extent of any interest they have in a material contract or material transaction with the bank holding company, whether entered into or proposed, if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are a party to the contract or transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: are a director or officer of a party to the contract or transaction or a person acting in a similar capacity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: have a material interest in a party to the contract or transaction.

Subsection.Text: The disclosure shall be made in the case of a director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, at which the proposed contract or transaction is first considered;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if at the time of the meeting referred to in paragraph (a) the director was not interested in the proposed contract or transaction, at the first one after they become interested in it;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the director becomes interested after a contract or transaction is entered into, at the first one after they become interested; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if a person who is interested in a contract or transaction becomes a director, at the first one after they become a director.

Subsection.Text: The disclosure required by subsection (1) shall be made in the case of an officer who is not a director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: immediately after they become aware that the contract, transaction, proposed contract or proposed transaction is to be considered or has been considered at a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if they become interested after the contract or transaction is entered into, immediately after they become interested; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a person who is interested in a contract or transaction becomes an officer, immediately after they become an officer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time of disclosure — contract not requiring approval
Subsection.Text: If the material contract or material transaction, whether entered into or proposed, is one that in the ordinary course of the bank holding company’s business would not require approval by the directors or shareholders, the director or officer shall disclose to the bank holding company, in writing or by requesting to have it entered in the minutes of a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors, the nature and extent of their interest immediately after they become aware of the contract or transaction.

Section.Text: Subsection 790(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director who is required to make a disclosure under subsection 789(1) shall not be present at any meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, while the contract or transaction is being considered or vote on any resolution to approve it unless the contract or transaction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relates primarily to their remuneration as a director, officer, employee or agent of the bank holding company, an entity controlled by the bank holding company or an entity in which the bank holding company has a substantial investment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is for indemnity under section 799 or insurance under section 800; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is with an affiliate of the bank holding company.

Section.Text: Sections 791 to 793 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 789(1), a general notice to the directors declaring that a director or officer is to be regarded as interested for any of the following reasons in a contract or transaction entered into with a party is a sufficient declaration of interest in relation to any contract or transaction with that party:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer is a director or officer of a party referred to in paragraph 789(1)(b) or (c) or a person acting in a similar capacity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer has a material interest in the party; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there has been a material change in the nature of the director’s or officer’s interest in the party.

Subsection.Text: The shareholders of the bank holding company may examine the portions of any minutes of meetings of directors or committees of directors that contain disclosures under subsection 789(1), or the portions of any other documents that contain those disclosures, during the usual business hours of the bank holding company.

A contract or transaction for which disclosure is required under subsection 789(1) is not invalid and a director or officer is not accountable to the bank holding company or its shareholders for any profit realized from it by reason only of the director’s or officer’s interest in the contract or transaction or the fact that the director was present or was counted to determine whether a quorum existed at the meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, that considered it if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer disclosed their interest in accordance with section 789 and subsection 791(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the directors approved the contract or transaction; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the bank holding company at the time that it was approved.

Subsection.Text: Even if the conditions set out in subsection (1) are not met, a director or officer acting honestly and in good faith is not accountable to the bank holding company or its shareholders for any profit realized from a contract or transaction for which disclosure was required and the contract or transaction is not invalid by reason only of the director’s or officer’s interest in it if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction is approved or confirmed by special resolution at a meeting of shareholders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disclosure of the interest was made to the shareholders in a manner sufficient to indicate its nature before the contract or transaction was approved or confirmed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the bank holding company at the time that it was approved or confirmed.

Section.MarginalNote: Court may set aside or require accounting
Section.Text: If a director or officer of a bank holding company fails to comply with any of sections 789 to 792, a court, on application of the bank holding company or any of its shareholders, may set aside the contract or transaction on any terms that the court thinks fit and may require the director or officer to account to the bank holding company for any profit or gain realized on it.
Section 794 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Directors of a bank holding company who vote for or consent to a resolution of the directors authorizing the issue of a share contrary to subsection 709(1) or the issue of subordinated indebtedness contrary to section 723 for a consideration other than money are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to the bank holding company to make good any amount by which the consideration received is less than the fair equivalent of the money that the bank holding company would have received if the share or subordinated indebtedness had been issued for money on the date of the resolution.

Directors of a bank holding company who vote for or consent to a resolution of the directors authorizing any of the following are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to restore to the bank holding company any amounts so distributed or paid and not otherwise recovered by the bank holding company and any amounts in relation to any loss suffered by the bank holding company:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a redemption or purchase of shares contrary to section 715;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reduction of capital contrary to section 718;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a payment of a dividend contrary to section 722; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a payment of an indemnity contrary to section 799.

Section.Text: Subsection 797(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the directors of a bank holding company are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to each employee of the bank holding company for all debts not exceeding six months wages payable to the employee for services performed for the bank holding company while they are directors.

Section.Text: Sections 798 and 799 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director, officer or employee of a bank holding company is not liable under section 794 or 797 and has fulfilled their duty under subsection 748(2) if they exercised the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would have exercised in comparable circumstances, including reliance in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the bank holding company that were represented to them by an officer of the bank holding company or in a written report of the auditor of the bank holding company fairly to reflect the financial condition of the bank holding company; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by them.

Subsection.Text: A director or officer of a bank holding company has fulfilled their duty under subsection 748(1) if they relied in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the bank holding company that were represented to them by an officer of the bank holding company or in a written report of the auditor of the bank holding company fairly to reflect the financial condition of the bank holding company; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by them.

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company may indemnify a director or officer of the bank holding company, a former director or officer of the bank holding company or another person who acts or acted, at the bank holding company’s request, as a director or officer of or in a similar capacity for another entity against all costs, charges and expenses, including an amount paid to settle an action or satisfy a judgment, reasonably incurred by them in respect of any civil, criminal, administrative, investigative or other proceeding in which they are involved because of that association with the bank holding company or other entity.

A bank holding company may advance amounts to the director, officer or other person for the costs, charges and expenses of a proceeding referred to in subsection (1). They shall repay the amounts if they do not fulfil the conditions set out in subsection (3).
A bank holding company may not indemnify a person under subsection (1) unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person acted honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of, as the case may be, the bank holding company or the other entity for which they acted at the bank holding company’s request as a director or officer or in a similar capacity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a criminal or administrative action or proceeding that is enforced by a monetary penalty, the person had reasonable grounds for believing that their conduct was lawful.

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company may with the approval of a court indemnify a person referred to in subsection (1) or advance amounts to them under subsection (2) — in respect of an action by or on behalf of the bank holding company or other entity to procure a judgment in its favour to which the person is made a party because of the association referred to in subsection (1) with the bank holding company or other entity — against all costs, charges and expenses reasonably incurred by them in connection with that action if they fulfil the conditions set out in subsection (3).

Despite subsection (1), a person referred to in that subsection is entitled to be indemnified by the bank holding company in respect of all costs, charges and expenses reasonably incurred by them in connection with the defence of any civil, criminal, administrative, investigative or other proceeding to which the person is subject because of the association referred to in subsection (1) with the bank holding company or other entity described in that subsection if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was not judged by the court or other competent authority to have committed any fault or omitted to do anything that they ought to have done; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fulfils the conditions set out in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company may, to the extent referred to in subsections (1) to (5) in respect of the person, indemnify the heirs or personal representatives of any person whom the bank holding company may indemnify under those subsections.

Section.Text: Paragraph 800(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the capacity of a director or officer of another entity or while acting in a similar capacity for another entity, if they act or acted in that capacity at the bank holding company’s request, except if the liability relates to a failure to act honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of the entity.

Section.Text: Paragraph 804(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the amalgamated bank holding company and the province in which its head office is to be situated;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 806(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Each share of an applicant carries the right to vote in respect of an amalgamation agreement whether or not it otherwise carries the right to vote.
Subsection 806(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Separate vote for class or series
Subsection.Text: The holders of shares of a class or series of shares of each applicant are entitled to vote separately as a class or series in respect of an amalgamation agreement if the agreement contains a provision that, if it were contained in a proposed amendment to the by-laws or incorporating instrument of the applicant, would entitle those holders to vote separately as a class or series.

Section.Text: Subsections 814(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company shall at all times have a head office in the province specified in its incorporating instrument or by-laws.
The directors of a bank holding company may change the address of the head office within the province specified in the incorporating instrument or by-laws.

Section.Text: Subsection 816(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Access to bank holding company records
Subsection.Text: Shareholders and creditors of a bank holding company and their personal representatives may examine the records referred to in subsection 815(1) during the usual business hours of the bank holding company and may take extracts from them free of charge or have copies of them made on payment of a reasonable fee. If the bank holding company is a distributing bank holding company, any other person may on payment of a reasonable fee examine those records during the usual business hours of the bank holding company and take extracts from them or have copies of them made.

Section.Text: Subsection 817(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A shareholder or creditor of a bank holding company or their personal representative — or if the bank holding company is a distributing bank holding company, any person — is entitled to a basic list of shareholders of the bank holding company.

Section.Text: Subsections 822(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Location and processing of information or data

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), a bank holding company shall maintain and process in Canada any information or data relating to the preparation or maintenance of the records referred to in section 815 or of its central securities register unless the Superintendent has, subject to any terms and conditions that the Superintendent considers appropriate, exempted the bank holding company from the application of this section.

Subject to subsections (3) and (4), the bank holding company may maintain copies of the records referred to in section 815 or of its central securities register outside Canada and may process outside Canada any information or data relating to those copies.

Section.Text: Subsection 825(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Shareholders and creditors of a bank holding company and their personal representatives may examine the central securities register during the usual business hours of the bank holding company and may take extracts from it free of charge or have copies of it made on payment of a reasonable fee. If the bank holding company is a distributing bank holding company, any other person may on payment of a reasonable fee examine the central securities register during the usual business hours of the bank holding company and take extracts from it or have copies of it made.

The bank holding company may make the information contained in the central securities register available by any mechanical or electronic data processing system or other information storage device that is capable of reproducing it in intelligible written form within a reasonable time.

A person who wishes to examine the central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made shall provide the bank holding company with an affidavit containing their name and address — or if they are an entity, the name and address for service of the entity — and with an undertaking that the information contained in the register will not be used except in the same way as a list of shareholders may be used under section 819. In the case of an entity, the affidavit is to be sworn by a director or officer of the entity or a person acting in a similar capacity.

A person who wishes to examine a central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the bank holding company or its agent to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplementary information to be provided
Subsection.Text: A bank holding company or its agent shall provide the supplementary information within

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 10 days after the day on which the central securities register is examined if the changes take place before that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 10 days after the day to which the supplementary information relates if the changes take place on or after the day on which the central securities register is examined.

Section.Text: Sections 833 to 835 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company may adopt a corporate seal and change one that it adopted.
A document executed on behalf of a bank holding company is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.

Section.Text: Sections 265 to 272 apply in respect of bank holding companies except that references to “bank” are to be read as references to “bank holding company”.
Sections 273 and 274 apply in respect of bank holding companies except that references to “bank” are to be read as references to “bank holding company”.
Sections 275 to 277 apply in respect of bank holding companies subject to the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: references to “bank” are to be read as references to “bank holding company”;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “this Act” in section 276 is to be read as a reference to “this Part”; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the reference to “a regulation referred to in subsection 485(1) or (2) or of an order made under subsection 485(3)” in subsection 277(25) is to be read as a reference to “a regulation referred to in subsection 949(1) or (2) or of an order made under subsection 949(3)”.

Section.Text: The portion of section 836 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Sections 283 to 292.1 apply in respect of bank holding companies, subject to the following:
Subsection 840(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: With respect to each of the financial years to which it relates, the annual statement of a bank holding company must contain the prescribed statements and any information that is in the opinion of the directors necessary to present fairly, in accordance with the accounting principles referred to in subsection (4), the financial position of the bank holding company as at the end of the financial year to which it relates and the results of the operations and changes in the financial position of the bank holding company for that financial year.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 841(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors of a bank holding company shall approve the annual statement and their approval shall be evidenced by the signature or a printed or otherwise mechanically reproduced facsimile of the signature of

Section.Text: Subsection 843(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company shall, no later than 21 days before the date of each annual meeting or before the signing of a resolution under paragraph 741(1)(b) in lieu of the annual meeting, send to each shareholder a copy of the documents referred to in subsections 840(1) and (3) unless that time period is waived by the shareholder.
The portion of paragraph 847(2)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member of a firm of accountants is deemed not to be independent of a bank holding company if that member, a business partner of that member or the firm of accountants

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a business partner, director, officer or employee of the bank holding company or of any affiliate of the bank holding company or is a business partner of any director, officer or employee of the bank holding company or of any affiliate of the bank holding company,

Subsection.Text: Section 847 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsection (2), a business partner of a member of a firm of accountants includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: another member of the firm; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a shareholder of the firm or of a business partner of the member.

Section.Text: Subsection 853(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In the case of a proposed replacement of an auditor whether because of removal or the expiry of their term, the bank holding company shall make a statement of the reasons for the proposed replacement and the proposed replacement auditor may make a statement in which they comment on those reasons.

The bank holding company shall send a copy of the statements referred to in subsections (1) and (1.1) without delay to every shareholder entitled to vote at the annual meeting of shareholders and to the Superintendent.

Section.Text: Subsection 860(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A bank holding company shall take all necessary steps to ensure that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: its auditor is the auditor of each of its subsidiaries; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a subsidiary with more than one auditor, the auditor of the bank holding company is one of the subsidiary’s auditors.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 965:

Any by-law, notice, resolution, requisition, statement or other document required or permitted to be executed or signed by more than one person for the purposes of this Act may be executed or signed in several documents of like form, each of which is executed or signed by one or more of the persons. The documents if duly executed or signed by all persons required or permitted to sign them are deemed to constitute one document for the purposes of this Act.
Subsection 967(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a bank or bank holding company sends a notice or document to a shareholder in accordance with section 965 and it is returned on two consecutive occasions because the shareholder cannot be found, the bank or bank holding company is not required to send any further notices or documents to the shareholder until it is informed in writing of their new address.

Section.Text: Section 970 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Mentions au registre des valeurs mobilières
Section.Text: Les mentions au registre des valeurs mobilières et sur les certificats de valeurs mobilières émis par la banque ou la société de portefeuille bancaire établissent que les personnes au nom desquelles les valeurs mobilières sont inscrites sont propriétaires des valeurs mentionnées dans le registre ou sur les certificats.
Section 978 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 978(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: The regulations may incorporate any material by reference regardless of its source and either as it exists on a particular date or as amended from time to time.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Incorporated material is not a regulation
Subsection.Text: Material does not become a regulation for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act because it is incorporated by reference.
Subparagraph 985(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 985(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person has been convicted of an offence under this Act, the court may, if it is satisfied that as a result of the commission of the offence the convicted person acquired any monetary benefits or that monetary benefits accrued to the convicted person or their spouse, common-law partner or other dependant, order the convicted person to pay, despite the maximum amount of any fine that may otherwise be imposed under this Act, an additional fine in an amount equal to three times the court’s estimation of the amount of those monetary benefits.

Section.Text: Section 990 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An appeal lies to the court of appeal of a province from any final order made by a court of that province under this Act.

An appeal lies to the court of appeal of a province from any order, other than a final order made by a court of that province, only with leave of the court of appeal in accordance with the rules applicable to that court.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 991:

Heading.Label: PART XVIII
Heading.TitleText: DOCUMENTS IN ELECTRONIC OR OTHER FORM

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: electronic document means, except in section 1001, any form of representation of information or concepts that is fixed in any medium in or by electronic, optical or other similar means and that can be read or perceived by a person or by any means.
information system means a system used to generate, send, receive, store or otherwise process an electronic document.

Section.Text: This Part other than sections 1004 and 1005 does not apply in respect of any notice, document or other information that under this Act or the regulations is sent to or issued by the Minister, the Superintendent, the Commissioner or the Bank of Canada or any prescribed notice, document or information.
Nothing in this Act or the regulations requires a person to create or provide an electronic document.

Subsection.Text: Despite anything in this Part, a requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a notice, document or other information is not satisfied by providing an electronic document unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the addressee consents and designates an information system for the receipt of the electronic document;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electronic document is, unless otherwise prescribed, provided to the designated information system; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed requirements are complied with.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the revocation of the consent referred to in paragraph (1)(a).

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act or the regulations to create or provide a notice, document or other information is satisfied by creating or providing an electronic document if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the incorporating instrument or by-laws of the bank, bank holding company or authorized foreign bank, as the case may be, do not provide otherwise; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed requirements are complied with.

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act or the regulations to create a notice, document or other information in writing is satisfied by creating an electronic document if in addition to the conditions set out in section 996

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information in the electronic document is accessible so as to be usable for subsequent reference; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed requirements are complied with.

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a notice, document or other information in writing is satisfied by providing an electronic document if in addition to the conditions set out in section 996

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information in the electronic document is accessible by the addressee and capable of being retained by them so as to be usable for subsequent reference; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed requirements are complied with.

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide two or more copies of a document at the same time to one addressee is satisfied by providing one copy of the electronic document.
A requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a document by registered mail is not satisfied by providing an electronic document except in the prescribed circumstances.

Subsection.Text: A statutory declaration or affidavit required under this Act or the regulations may be created or provided in an electronic document if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person who makes the statutory declaration or affidavit signs it with their secure electronic signature;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the authorized person before whom the statutory declaration or affidavit is made signs it with their secure electronic signature; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the requirements of sections 994 to 1000 are complied with.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, “electron­ic document” and “secure electronic signature” have the same meaning as in subsection 31(1) of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act.

For the purpose of complying with paragraph (1)(c), references to “electronic document” in sections 994 to 1000 are to be read as references to “electronic document within the meaning of subsection 31(1) of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act”.

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act or the regulations for a signature or for a document to be executed, except in respect of a statutory declaration or affidavit, is satisfied in respect of an electronic document if the prescribed requirements are complied with and the signature results from the application by the person of a technology or process that permits the following to be proved:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the signature resulting from the use by the person of the technology or process is unique to the person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the technology or process is used by the person to incorporate their signature into, attach it to or associate it with the electronic document; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the technology or process can be used to identify the person using the technology or process.

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations — provision and receipt of documents
Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the time and place at which and the circumstances under which an electronic document is considered to be provided or received.

Section.MarginalNote: Content and form of notices and documents
Section.Text: The Minister, Superintendent, Commissioner or Bank of Canada may establish the requirements for the content and fix the form, including electronic and other forms, of notices and documents sent to or issued by each of them under this Act or the regulations, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the notices and documents that may be sent in electronic or other form;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons or classes of persons who may send notices and documents;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: their signature in electronic or other form or their execution, adoption or authorization in a manner that is to have the same effect for the purposes of this Act as their signature;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the time and place at which and the circumstances under which electronic documents are considered to be sent or received; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any matter necessary for the purposes of the application of this section.

Section.Text: In the prescribed circumstances, the Minister, Superintendent, Commissioner or Bank of Canada may, on any conditions that they consider appropriate, exempt from the application of any provision of this Act requiring a notice or document to be sent to them any notice or document, or class of notice or document, containing information similar to that contained in a notice or document required to be made public under any other Act of Parliament or any Act of the legislature of a province.

Section.MarginalNote: Canada Gazette, Part I, Vol. 139, No. 11, p. 751
Section.Text: Schedules I to III of the Act are replaced by the schedules set out in the schedule to this Act.

Subsection.Text: The definitions form of proxy and proxy in section 2 of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: form of proxy means a form of proxy as defined in the regulations;
proxy means a proxy as defined in the regulations;

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: minor has the same meaning as in the applicable provincial law and in the absence of any such law has the same meaning as the word “child” in the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child adopted in the United Nations General Assembly on November 20, 1989;

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: association n’ayant pas fait appel au public

Definition.Text: association n’ayant pas fait appel au public S’entend d’une association autre qu’une association ayant fait appel au public.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 3:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the determination of what constitutes a distributing association for the purposes of this Act.

On the application of an association, the Superintendent may determine that it is not or was not a distributing association if the Superintendent is satisfied that the determination would not prejudice any of its security holders.

The Superintendent may determine that members of a class of associations are not or were not distributing associations if the Superintendent is satisfied that the determination would not prejudice any security holder of a member of the class.

Section.Text: Section 13 of the Act is repealed.
Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No association and no guarantor of an obligation of an association may assert against a person dealing with the association or against a person who has acquired rights from the association that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the association’s incorporating instrument or any by-laws of the association have not been complied with;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons named as directors of the association in the most recent return sent to the Superintendent under section 432 are not the directors of the association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the place named in the incorporating instrument or by-laws of the association is not the place where the head office of the association is situated;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person held out by the association as a director, officer or representative of the association has not been duly appointed or has no authority to exercise the powers and perform the duties that are customary in the business of the association or usual for a director, officer or representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
