Paragraph.Text: sections 160 to 162, paragraphs 165(2)(a) to (d), (h) and (i), sections 166 and 167, subsection 168(1), sections 170 to 172, subsections 174(1) and (3) to (6), 174(7) (excepting therefrom the reference to subsection 173(4)) and 175(1) and (4), sections 177 and 189 to 194, subsection 195(1), sections 196, 202 and 203, subsections 204(1) and (2), paragraphs 204(3)(a) to (c), subsections 204(4) to (6), sections 205 and 206, paragraphs 207(a) to (c), (h) and (i), sections 208 to 215, paragraphs 216(2)(d) and (e), subsections 217(1) and (2), paragraphs 217(3)(a) and (c), sections 218 to 223, 244, 254 to 256 and 260, subsections 261(1) and (2) and 262(1) to (6), sections 266 to 268, paragraphs 269(a) and (b), sections 270, 278, 279 and 330, paragraphs 331(1)(a) and (b) to (e), subsection 331(2), paragraphs 331(3)(b) and (c), subsections 331(4) and (6), sections 332 to 357 and 359.1 to 380, paragraph 381(1)(a), subsection 381(2) and sections 382 to 406 of Part VI;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 17(1)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: Part XI, except sections 528.1 to 528.3, and Parts XV, XVI and XVIII to XX.

Subsection.Text: The definitions form of proxy and proxy in subsection 2(1) of the Insurance Companies Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: form of proxy means a form of proxy as defined in the regulations;
proxy means a proxy as defined in the regulations;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: adjustable policy means an adjustable policy as defined in the regulations;
going-private transaction means a going-private transaction as defined in the regulations;

minor has the same meaning as in the applicable provincial law and in the absence of any such law has the same meaning as the word “child” in the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child adopted in the United Nations General Assembly on November 20, 1989;

squeeze-out transaction means a transaction by a company that is not a distributing company, or an insurance holding company that is not a distributing insurance holding company, that requires an amendment to a by-law referred to in subsection 238(1) or 851(1), as the case may be, and that would directly or indirectly result in the interest of a holder of shares of a class of shares being terminated without their consent and without substituting an interest of equivalent value in shares that are issued by the following persons and have rights and privileges equal to or greater than those of the shares of the affected class:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a company, the company, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an insurance holding company, the insurance holding company;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: société de portefeuille d’assurances n’ayant pas fait appel au public

Definition.Text: société de portefeuille d’assurances n’ayant pas fait appel au public S’entend d’une société de portefeuille d’assurances autre qu’une société de portefeuille d’assurances ayant fait appel au public.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: société n’ayant pas fait appel au public

Definition.Text: société n’ayant pas fait appel au public S’entend d’une société autre qu’une société ayant fait appel au public.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 3:

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations — distributing company or insurance holding company

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the determination of what constitutes a distributing company or distributing insurance holding company for the purposes of this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exemption — company or insurance holding company
Subsection.Text: On the application of a company or insurance holding company, the Superintendent may determine that it is not or was not a distributing company or distributing insurance holding company, as the case may be, if the Superintendent is satisfied that the determination would not prejudice any security holder of that company or insurance holding company.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exemption — class of companies or insurance holding companies
Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may determine that members of a class of companies or insurance holding companies are not or were not distributing companies or distributing insurance holding companies, as the case may be, if the Superintendent is satisfied that the determination would not prejudice any security holder of a member of the class.

Section.Text: Section 11 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 13(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This Part, Parts II to IV, sections 224, 225, 245 to 258 and 489 and Parts X, XII, XV, XVI and XVIII to XX apply to every body corporate that is not discontinued under this Act and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is incorporated or continued as a society under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to which any of the provisions of Parts I and II, Part III, except section 77, Part IV, except sections 123 to 130 and 153 to 158, and Parts V and VII of the Canadian and British Insurance Companies Act applied before June 1, 1992.

Section.Text: Section 20 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No company or society and no guarantor of an obligation of a company or society may assert against a person dealing with the company or society or against a person who has acquired rights from the company or society that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company’s or society’s incorporating instrument or any by-laws of the company or society have not been complied with;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons named as directors of the company or society in the most recent return sent to the Superintendent under section 549 or 668 are not the directors of the company or society;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the place named in the incorporating instrument or by-laws of the company or society is not the place where the head office of the company or society is situated;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person held out by the company or society as a director, officer or representative of the company or society has not been duly appointed or has no authority to exercise the powers and perform the duties that are customary in the business of the company or society or usual for a director, officer or representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a document issued by any director, officer or representative of the company or society with actual or usual authority to issue the document is not valid or not genuine.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a person who has or ought to have knowledge of a situation described in that subsection by virtue of their relationship to the company or society.

Section.Text: Paragraph 28(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the province in which the head office of the company or society is to be situated;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The by-laws of a company may, subject to any limitations set out in them, authorize the issue of any class of shares in one or more series and may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fix the maximum number of shares in each series and determine the designation, rights, privileges, restrictions and conditions attaching to them; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the directors to do anything referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the directors exercise their authority under paragraph (1)(b), the directors shall, before the issue of shares of the series, send to the Superintendent a copy of the by-law authorizing the directors to fix the rights, privileges, restrictions and conditions of those shares and shall provide the Superintendent with particulars of the proposed series of shares.

Section.Text: Subsection 70(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), a company may, subject to subsection (2.2), record in the stated capital account maintained for the shares of a class or series any part of the consideration it receives in an exchange if it issues shares

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in exchange for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who immediately before the exchange did not deal with the company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of or another interest in a body corporate that immediately before the exchange or because of it did not deal with the company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who immediately before the exchange dealt with the company at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act if the person, the company and all of the holders of shares in the class or series of shares so issued consent to the exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement referred to in subsection 246(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to shareholders of an amalgamating body corporate who receive the shares in addition to or instead of securities of the amalgamated company.

Section.Text: Subsection 76(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La société — ainsi que ses filiales si elle le leur permet — peut, en qualité de représentant personnel, mais à condition de ne pas en avoir la propriété effective, détenir soit des actions de la société ou d’une personne morale qui la contrôle, soit des titres de participation d’une entité non constituée en personne morale qui la contrôle.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 76:

Subsection.Text: A company may permit its subsidiary to acquire shares of the company, shares of an entity that controls the company or any ownership interests of any unincorporated entity that controls the company if before the subsidiary acquires them the conditions prescribed for the purposes of this subsection are met.
After a subsidiary has acquired shares or ownership interests in accordance with subsection (1), the conditions prescribed for the purposes of this subsection are to be met.
Despite section 16 and subsection 70(2), the issue and acquisition of the shares or ownership interests are subject to the prescribed requirements if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company permits the subsidiary to acquire the shares or ownership interests; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a condition prescribed for the purposes of subsection (1) was not met, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a condition prescribed for the purposes of subsection (2) was not met or ceased to be met.

Section.Text: Section 90 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A security certificate shall be signed by or bear the printed or otherwise mechanically reproduced signature of at least one of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registrar or transfer agent of the company or a branch transfer agent or a natural person on their behalf; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee who certifies it in accordance with a trust indenture.

Subsection.Text: If a security certificate contains a person’s printed or mechanically reproduced signature, the company may issue the security certificate even if the person has ceased to be a director or officer of the company. The security certificate is as valid as if the person were a director or officer at the date of its issue.

Section.Text: Section 92 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No charge in favour of a company and no restriction on transfer, other than a constraint under Part VII other than section 427, is effective against a transferee of a security issued by the company if the transferee has no actual knowledge of the charge or restriction unless it or a reference to it is noted conspicuously on the security certificate.

If any of the issued shares of a distributing company remain outstanding and are held by more than one person, the company may not restrict the transfer or ownership of its shares except by way of a constraint under Part VII.

If a body corporate that is continued as a company under this Act has outstanding security certificates and the words “private company” or “private corporation” appear on the certificates, those words are deemed to be a notice of a charge or restriction for the purposes of subsection (1).

Section.Text: Paragraph 97(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the personal representative of a registered security holder who is a minor, an incompetent person or a missing person; or

Section.Text: Section 98 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
If a minor exercises any rights of ownership in the securities of a company, no subsequent repudiation or avoidance is effective against the company.
Section 140 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 140(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any person who is entitled to attend a meeting of shareholders or policyholders may participate in the meeting by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting if the company makes one available. A person who is participating in a meeting by one of those means is deemed for the purposes of this Act to be present at the meeting.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the manner of and conditions for participating in a meeting by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that permits all participants to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting.

Section.Text: Section 141 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 141(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Order to delay calling annual meeting
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the company may apply to the court for an order extending the time for calling an annual meeting.
The company shall give notice of the application to the Superintendent before any hearing concerning the application and shall provide the Superintendent with a copy of any order that is issued.
The Superintendent is entitled to appear and be heard in person or by counsel at any hearing concerning the application.

Section.Text: Section 142 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors may in advance fix a record date, that is within the prescribed period, for the determination of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shareholders who are entitled to receive payment of a dividend;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shareholders or policyholders who are entitled to participate in a liquidation distribution;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shareholders

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: who are entitled to receive notice of a meeting of shareholders or a meeting of shareholders and policyholders,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: who are entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders or at a meeting of shareholders and policyholders, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for any other purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: policyholders who are entitled to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: receive notice of a meeting of policyholders or a meeting of shareholders and policyholders, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: vote at a meeting of policyholders or at a meeting of shareholders and policyholders; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: policyholders for any other purpose except

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the right to receive payment of a policy dividend or bonus,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any purpose where the determination of policyholders is governed by contract, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the right to receive benefits in respect of the conversion of a mutual company into a company with common shares.

Subsection.Text: If no record date is fixed,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of shareholders who are entitled to receive notice of a meeting of shareholders or a meeting of shareholders and policyholders is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the close of business on the day immediately preceding the day on which the notice is given, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice is given, the day on which the meeting is held; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of shareholders for any other purpose, other than to establish a shareholder’s right to vote, is at the close of business on the day on which the directors pass a resolution in respect of that purpose.

Subsection.Text: If no record date is fixed,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of policyholders who are entitled to receive notice of a meeting of policyholders or a meeting of shareholders and policyholders is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the close of business on the day immediately preceding the day on which the notice is given, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice is given, the day on which the meeting is held;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of policyholders entitled to vote at a meeting is the day on which the meeting is held; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the record date for the determination of policyholders for any other purpose is at the close of business on the day on which the directors pass a resolution in respect of that purpose.

Subsection.Text: If a record date is fixed and unless notice of the record date is waived in writing by every holder of a share of the class or series affected whose name is set out in the securities register at the close of business on the day on which the directors fix the record date, notice of the record date shall be given within the prescribed period by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: advertisement in a newspaper in general circulation in the place where the company’s head office is situated and in each place in Canada where the company has a transfer agent or where a transfer of its shares may be recorded; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: written notice to each stock exchange in Canada on which the company’s shares are listed for trading.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 143(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Notice of the time and place of a meeting of a company’s shareholders or policyholders shall be sent within the prescribed period to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each shareholder entitled to vote at the meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each policyholder who is determined under subsection (1.4) or (1.6) to be entitled to notice if no business referred to in subparagraphs (c)(i) to (iii) is to be dealt with;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each policyholder entitled to vote at the meeting if any of the following business is to be dealt with:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: authorizing the company to apply to the Minister for approval of a mutualization proposal or amalgamation agreement,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: confirming a by-law changing the rights of policyholders to vote at meetings or the province in which the head office of the company is situated, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: approving an agreement setting out the terms and means of effecting the transfer of all or substantially all of the company’s policies or the reinsurance of all or substantially all of the company’s policies;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: each director;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the actuary of the company; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent.

Subsection.Text: In the case of a company that is not a distributing company and does not have any policyholders entitled to vote, notice may be sent within any shorter period specified in its by-laws.
Subsection 143(1.6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a company chooses the practice under subsection (1.5), each policyholder entitled to vote at a meeting is entitled to notice under paragraph (1)(b) if, within three years before the record date fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(d)(i) or determined under paragraph 142(3)(a), they complete and return to the company the form referred to in paragraph (1.5)(b) indicating that they want to receive notice or they indicate to the satisfaction of the company that they want to receive notice.
Subsections 143(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Notice of a meeting is not required to be sent to shareholders who are not registered on the records of the company or the company’s transfer agent on the record date fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(i) or determined under paragraph 142(2)(a).

Notice of a meeting at which business referred to in paragraph (1)(c) is to be dealt with is not required to be sent to policyholders who become policyholders after the record date fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(d)(i) or determined under paragraph 142(3)(a).
Subsection 147(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (1.1) and (1.2), a registered holder or beneficial owner of shares that may be voted — or a policyholder entitled to vote — at an annual meeting of shareholders and policyholders may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: submit to the company notice of any matter that they propose to raise at the meeting (in this section and section 148 referred to as a “proposal”); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: discuss at the meeting any matter in respect of which they would have been entitled to submit a proposal.

Subsection.Text: To be eligible to submit a proposal a person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for at least the prescribed period be the registered holder or beneficial owner of at least the prescribed number of the company’s outstanding shares;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have the support of persons who, in the aggregate and including or not including the person who submits the proposal, have for at least the prescribed period been the registered holders or beneficial owners of at least the prescribed number of the company’s outstanding shares; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: be a policyholder entitled to vote at an annual meeting of the company’s shareholders and policyholders.

Subsection.Text: A proposal submitted by a registered holder or beneficial owner is to be accompanied by the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of the person submitting the proposal and the names and addresses of their supporters, if any; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of shares held or owned by the person and their supporters, if any, and the date that the shares were acquired.

Subsection.Text: The information provided under subsection (1.2) does not form part of a proposal or of the supporting statement referred to in subsection (3) and is not to be included for the purpose of the prescribed maximum number of words referred to in subsection (3).

If the company requests within the prescribed period that a person provide proof that they are eligible to submit a proposal, the person shall within the prescribed period provide proof that they meet the requirements of subsection (1.1).
Subsections 147(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

At the request of the person who submits a proposal, the company shall attach to the notice of the meeting the person’s statement in support of the proposal and their name and address. The statement and proposal together are not to exceed the prescribed maximum number of words.
A proposal may include nominations for the election of directors if it is signed by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of nominations for the directors to be elected by shareholders, one or more registered holders or beneficial owners of shares representing in the aggregate not less than 5% of the shares of the company or 5% of the shares of a class of its shares entitled to vote at the meeting at which the proposal is to be presented; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of nominations for the directors to be elected by policyholders, the lesser of 250 policyholders and 1% of the policyholders who are entitled to vote at the meeting.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 147(5)(a) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proposal is not submitted to the company at least the prescribed number of days before the anniversary date of the notice of meeting that was sent to shareholders and policyholders in respect of the previous annual meeting of shareholders and policyholders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it clearly appears that the primary purpose of the proposal is to enforce a personal claim or redress a personal griev­ance against the company or its directors, officers or security holders;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: it clearly appears that the proposal does not relate in a significant way to the business or affairs of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person submitting the proposal failed within the prescribed period before the company receives their proposal to present, in person or by proxy, at a meeting of shareholders or policyholders a proposal that at their request had been attached to a notice of meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: substantially the same proposal was set out in or attached to a dissident’s proxy circular or attached to a notice of meeting relating to, and was presented to shareholders or policyholders at, a meeting of shareholders or policyholders held within the prescribed period before the receipt of the proposal and did not receive the prescribed minimum amount of support at the meeting;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the rights conferred by subsections (1) to (4) are being abused to secure publicity;

Subsection.Text: Section 147 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Company may refuse to include proposal
Subsection.Text: If a person who submits a proposal fails to continue to hold or own shares in accordance with paragraph (1.1)(a) or, as the case may be, does not continue to have the support of persons who are in the aggregate the registered holders or beneficial owners of the prescribed number of shares in accordance with paragraph (1.1)(b) until the end of the meeting, the company is not required to attach any proposal submitted by that person to a notice of meeting for any meeting held within the prescribed period after the day of the meeting.
Subsections 148(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a company refuses to attach a proposal to a notice of a meeting, it shall in writing notify the person submitting the proposal of its intention not to attach the proposal to the notice of the meeting and of the reasons for the refusal. It shall notify the person within the prescribed period after either the day on which it receives the proposal or, if it has requested proof under subsection 147(1.4), the day on which it receives the proof.

On the application of a person submitting a proposal who claims to be aggrieved by a company’s refusal under subsection (1), a court may restrain the holding of the meeting at which the proposal is sought to be presented and make any further order that it thinks fit.
Subsection 148(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

La société ou toute personne qui prétend qu’une proposition lui cause un préjudice peut demander au tribunal une ordonnance autorisant la société à ne pas l’annexer à l’avis de l’assemblée; le tribunal, s’il est convaincu que le paragraphe 147(5) s’applique, peut rendre en l’espèce la décision qu’il estime pertinente.
Subsections 149(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:
A company shall prepare an alphabetical list

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of shareholders entitled to receive notice of a meeting showing the number of shares held by each shareholder

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a record date is fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(i), no later than 10 days after that date, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no record date is fixed, on the record date determined under paragraph 142(2)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of policyholders entitled to vote at a meeting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a record date is fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(d)(ii), no later than the day on which the meeting is held, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no record date is fixed, on the record date determined under paragraph 142(3)(b).

Subsection.Text: The company shall prepare an alphabetical list of shareholders entitled to vote as of the record date showing the number of shares held by each shareholder

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a record date is fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(ii), no later than 10 days after that date; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if no record date is fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(ii), no later than 10 days after a record date is fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(i) or no later than the record date determined under paragraph 142(2)(a), as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 164.08, a shareholder whose name appears on a list prepared under subsection (1.1) is entitled to vote the shares shown opposite their name.
A policyholder whose name appears on a list prepared under paragraph (1)(b) is entitled to vote at the meeting to which the list relates.
The portion of subsection 149(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A shareholder or policyholder may examine the list of shareholders

Section.Text: Section 157 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1) and unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any vote referred to in that subsection may be held entirely by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility if the company makes one available.

Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, any person who is participating in a meeting of shareholders or policyholders under subsection 140(2) and entitled to vote at that meeting may vote by means of the telephonic, electronic or other communication facility that the company has made available for that purpose.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the manner of and conditions for voting at a meeting of shareholders or policyholders by means of a telephonic, electronic or other communication facility.

Section.Text: Section 158 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting that the chairperson declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is in the absence of evidence to the contrary proof of that fact without proof of the number or proportion of votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.

Section.Text: Paragraph 159(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a record date has been fixed under subparagraph 142(1)(c)(i) or (d)(i) and notice of it has been given under subsection 142(4);

Section.Text: Subsections 160(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Court may order meeting to be called

Subsection.Text: A court may, on the application of a director, a shareholder or policyholder who is entitled to vote or the Superintendent, order a meeting to be called, held or conducted in the manner that the court directs if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is impracticable to call the meeting within the time or in the manner in which it is to be called;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is impracticable to conduct the meeting in the manner required by this Act or the by-laws; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the court thinks that the meeting ought to be called, held or conducted within the time or in the manner that it directs for any other reason.

Subsection.Text: The definition registrant in section 164 of the Act is repealed.
The definition “solicit or solicitation” in section 164 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a request for a proxy whether or not accompanied by a form of proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a request to execute or not to execute a form of proxy or to revoke a proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy or other communication to a shareholder or policyholder under circumstances reasonably calculated to result in the procurement, withholding or revocation of a proxy, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy to a shareholder or policyholder under section 164.03; but

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: does not include

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending of a form of proxy in response to an unsolicited request made by or on behalf of a shareholder or policyholder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the performance of administrative acts or professional services on behalf of a person soliciting a proxy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the sending by an intermediary of the documents referred to in subsection 164.06(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a solicitation by a person in respect of shares of which they are the beneficial owner,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed public announcement by a shareholder or policyholder of how they intend to vote and the reasons for that decision,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a communication for the purposes of obtaining the support of persons in accord­ance with paragraph 147(1.1)(b), or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a communication, other than a solic­itation by or on behalf of the management of a company, that is made to shareholders or policyholders in the prescribed circumstances.

Subsection.Text: Section 164 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: intermediary means a person who holds a security on behalf of another person who is not the registered holder of the security, and includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a securities broker or dealer required to be registered to trade or deal in securities under the laws of any jurisdiction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a securities depositary;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a clearing agency, a securities dealer, trust company, association within the meaning of section 2 of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, bank or other person, including another clearing agency, on whose behalf the clearing agency or its nominee holds securities of an issuer;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee or administrator of a self-administered retirement savings plan, retirement income fund or education savings plan or another similar self-administered savings or investment plan registered under the Income Tax Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a nominee of a person referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a person who performs functions similar to those performed by a person referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) and holds a security registered in their name, or in the name of their nominee, on behalf of another person who is not the registered holder of the security.

Section.Text: Subsection 164.03(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The management of a company is not required to send a form of proxy to shareholders under subsection (1) if the company

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not a distributing company; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has 50 or fewer shareholders who are entitled to vote at a meeting, two or more joint holders of a share being counted as one shareholder.

Section.Text: Section 164.04 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph (1)(b), it is not necessary to send a dissident’s proxy circular if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total number of shareholders and policyholders whose proxies are solicited is 15 or fewer, two or more joint holders of a share being counted as one shareholder; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the solicitation is conveyed by public broadcast, speech or publication and the prescribed requirements are complied with.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 164.05(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) and (2) and unless a shareholder, policyholder or proxyholder demands a ballot, if the chairperson of a meeting of shareholders or policyholders declares to the meeting that, if a ballot were conducted, the total number of votes represented at the meeting by proxy required to be voted against what, to the knowledge of the chairperson, would be the decision of the meeting on a matter or group of matters is less than 5% of all the votes that might be cast by shareholders or policyholders, in person or by proxy,

Section.Text: Section 164.06 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Shares of a company that are registered in the name of an intermediary or an intermediary’s nominee and not beneficially owned by the intermediary may not be voted unless the intermediary sends to the beneficial owner

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a copy of the notice of the meeting, annual statement, management proxy circular and dissident’s proxy circular and any other documents, other than the form of proxy, that were sent to shareholders by or on behalf of any person for use in connection with the meeting; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a written request for voting instructions except if the intermediary has already received written voting instructions from the beneficial owner.

Subsection.Text: The intermediary shall send the documents referred to in subsection (1) without delay after they receive the documents referred to in paragraph (1)(a).

An intermediary or a proxyholder appointed by them may not vote shares that the intermediary does not beneficially own and that are registered in the name of the intermediary or their nominee unless the intermediary or proxyholder, as the case may be, receives written voting instructions from the beneficial owner.

A person by or on behalf of whom a solicitation is made shall on request and without delay provide the intermediary, at the person’s expense, with the necessary number of copies of the documents referred to in paragraph (1)(a).
The intermediary shall vote or appoint a proxyholder to vote in accordance with any written voting instructions received from the beneficial owner.
If a beneficial owner so requests and provides an intermediary with appropriate documentation, the intermediary shall appoint the beneficial owner or a nominee of the beneficial owner as proxyholder.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Effect of intermediary’s failure to comply
Subsection.Text: The failure of an intermediary to comply with any of subsections (1) to (6) does not render void any meeting of shareholders or policyholders or any action taken at the meeting.
Nothing in this Division gives an intermediary the right to vote shares that they are otherwise prohibited from voting.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the conditions under which a company is exempt from any of the requirements of sections 164.01 to 164.06.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 165(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: establish a policy respecting the management of each of the participating accounts maintained under section 456,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the company has participating policyholders on the day on which this paragraph comes into force, within six months after that day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, before issuing a participating policy;

Paragraph.Label: (e.2)
Paragraph.Text: establish criteria for changes made by the company to the premium or charge for insurance, amount of insurance or surrender value in respect of its adjustable policies,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the company has adjustable policyholders on the day on which this paragraph comes into force, within six months after that day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, before issuing an adjustable policy;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 165(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The company’s actuary shall report to the directors in writing on the fairness to participating policyholders of any policy to be established or amended under paragraph (2)(e) and report at least once during each financial year on its continuing fairness. In the case of a policy established before the day on which this subsection comes into force, the actuary shall within six months after that day report on its fairness to participating policyholders and report at least once during each financial year on its continuing fairness.

The company’s actuary shall report to the directors in writing on the fairness to participating policyholders of any policy to be established or amended under paragraph (2)(e.1) and report at least once during each financial year on its continuing fairness.

The company’s actuary shall report to the directors in writing on the fairness to adjustable policyholders of the criteria to be established or amended under paragraph (2)(e.2) and report at least once during each financial year on their continuing fairness.

The directors shall consider the report prepared under subsection (3.1), (3.2) or (3.3) before establishing or amending a policy under paragraph (2)(e) or (e.1) or the criteria under paragraph (2)(e.2), as the case may be.

The report of the actuary referred to in subsections (3.1) to (3.3) shall be prepared in accordance with generally accepted actuarial practice with such changes as may be determined by the Superintendent and any additional directions that may be made by the Superintendent.

The company shall, within 30 days after a policy is established or amended under paragraph (2)(e) or (e.1) or criteria are established or amended under paragraph (2)(e.2), send a copy to the Superintendent.

The company shall on request and free of charge send a copy of a policy established or amended under paragraph (2)(e) or (e.1) to a shareholder or participating policyholder and on payment of a reasonable fee to any other person.
The company shall on request and free of charge send a copy of the criteria referred to in paragraph (2)(e.2) to an adjustable policyholder and on payment of a reasonable fee to any other person.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the content of the policies established under paragraphs (2)(e) and (e.1) and of the criteria established under paragraph (2)(e.2).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 173:
The election or appointment of a person as a director is subject to the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person was present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place and did not refuse to hold office as a director; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person was not present at the meeting when the election or appointment took place but

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: consented in writing to hold office as a director before the election or appointment or within 10 days after it, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: acted as a director after the election or appointment.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 176(1)(g) and (h) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a director elected by cumulative voting may be removed from office only if the number of votes cast in favour of a motion to remove the director is greater than the product of the number of directors required by the by-laws to be elected by cumulative voting and the number of votes cast against the motion; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the number of directors required by the by-laws to be elected by cumulative voting may be decreased only if the number of votes cast in favour of a motion to decrease the number of directors is greater than the product of the number of directors required by the by-laws to be elected by cumulative voting and the number of votes cast against the motion.

Section.Text: Subsection 185(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 192 but subject to subsection (2) and sections 184, 186 and 187, a quorum of directors may fill a vacancy among the directors except a vacancy resulting from a change in the by-laws by which the number or the minimum or maximum number of directors is increased or from a failure to elect the number or minimum number of directors provided for in the by-laws.

Section.Text: Paragraph 186(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the remaining shareholders’ directors or policyholders’ directors, as the case may be, may fill the vacancy except one resulting from an increase in the number or the minimum or maximum number — or from a failure to elect the number or minimum number — of shareholders’ directors or policyholders’ directors provided for in the by-laws;

Section.Text: Paragraph 187(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the remaining directors elected by the holders of that class or series of shares may fill the vacancy except one resulting from an increase in the number or the minimum or maximum number of directors for that class or series or from a failure to elect the number or minimum number of directors provided for in the by-laws for that class or series;

Section.Text: Subsection 191(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director who is present at a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors but is not, in accordance with subsection 212(1), present at any particular time during the meeting is considered to be present for the purposes of this section.

Section.Text: Section 193.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: Unless a ballot is demanded, an entry in the minutes of a meeting that the chairperson declared a resolution to be carried or defeated is in the absence of evidence to the contrary proof of that fact without proof of the number or proportion of votes recorded in favour of or against the resolution.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 207(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fill a vacancy among the directors, on a committee of directors or in the office of auditor or actuary, or appoint additional directors;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: issue or cause to be issued securities, including an issue of shares of a series that is authorized in accordance with section 66, except in accordance with any authorization made by the directors;

Section.Text: Section 211 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director or officer of a company shall disclose to the company, in writing or by requesting to have it entered in the minutes of a meeting of directors or a meeting of a committee of directors, the nature and extent of any interest they have in a material contract or material transaction with the company, whether entered into or proposed, if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are a party to the contract or transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: are a director or officer of a party to the contract or transaction or a person acting in a similar capacity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: have a material interest in a party to the contract or transaction.

Subsection.Text: The disclosure shall be made in the case of a director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, at which the proposed contract or transaction is first considered;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if at the time of the meeting referred to in paragraph (a) the director was not interested in the proposed contract or transaction, at the first one after they become interested in it;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the director becomes interested after a contract or transaction is entered into, at the first one after they become interested; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if a person who is interested in a contract or transaction becomes a director, at the first one after they become a director.

Subsection.Text: The disclosure shall be made in the case of an officer who is not a director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: immediately after they become aware that the contract, transaction, proposed contract or proposed transaction is to be considered or has been considered at a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if they become interested after the contract or transaction is entered into, immediately after they become interested; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a person who is interested in a contract or transaction becomes an officer, immediately after they become an officer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time of disclosure — contract not requiring approval
Subsection.Text: If the material contract or material transaction, whether entered into or proposed, is one that in the ordinary course of the company’s business would not require approval by the directors, shareholders or policyholders, the director or officer shall disclose to the company, in writing or by requesting to have it entered in the minutes of a meeting of directors or of a committee of directors, the nature and extent of their interest immediately after they become aware of the contract or transaction.

Section.Text: Subsection 212(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director who is required to make a disclosure under subsection 211(1) shall not be present at any meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, while the contract or transaction is being considered or vote on any resolution to approve it unless the contract or transaction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relates primarily to their remuneration as a director, officer, employee or agent of the company, an entity controlled by the company or an entity in which the company has a substantial investment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is for indemnity under section 221 or insurance under section 222; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is with an affiliate of the company.

Section.Text: Sections 213 to 215 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 211(1), a general notice to the directors declaring that a director or officer is to be regarded as interested for any of the following reasons in a contract or transaction entered into with a party is a sufficient declaration of interest in relation to any contract or transaction with that party:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer is a director or officer of a party referred to in paragraph 211(1)(b) or (c) or a person acting in a similar capacity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer has a material interest in the party; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there has been a material change in the nature of the director’s or officer’s interest in the party.

Subsection.Text: A policyholder who is entitled to vote or a shareholder may examine the portions of any minutes of meetings of directors or committees of directors that contain disclosures under subsection 211(1), or the portions of any other documents that contain those disclosures, during the usual business hours of the company.

A contract or transaction for which disclosure is required under subsection 211(1) is not invalid and a director or officer is not accountable to the company or its shareholders or policyholders for any profit realized from it by reason only of the director’s or officer’s interest in the contract or transaction or the fact that the director was present or was counted to determine whether a quorum existed at the meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, that considered it if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the director or officer disclosed their interest in accordance with section 211 and subsection 213(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the directors approved the contract or transaction; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the company at the time that it was approved.

Subsection.Text: Even if the conditions set out in subsection (1) are not met, a director or officer acting honestly and in good faith is not accountable to the company or its shareholders or policyholders for any profit realized from a contract or transaction for which disclosure was required and the contract or transaction is not invalid by reason only of the director’s or officer’s interest in it if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction is approved or confirmed by special resolution at a meeting of shareholders and policyholders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disclosure of the interest was made to the shareholders and to the policyholders entitled to vote in a manner sufficient to indicate its nature before the contract or transaction was approved or confirmed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contract or transaction was reasonable and fair to the company at the time that it was approved or confirmed.

Section.MarginalNote: Court may set aside or require accounting
Section.Text: If a director or officer of a company fails to comply with any of sections 211 to 214, a court, on application of the company or any of its policyholders entitled to vote or its shareholders, may set aside the contract or transaction on any terms that the court thinks fit and may require the director or officer to account to the company for any profit or gain realized on it.
Section 216 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Directors of a company who vote for or consent to a resolution of the directors authorizing the issue of a share contrary to subsection 69(1) or the issue of subordinated indebtedness contrary to section 84 for a consideration other than money are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to the company to make good any amount by which the consideration received is less than the fair equivalent of the money that the company would have received if the share or subordinated indebtedness had been issued for money on the date of the resolution.

Directors of a company who vote for or consent to a resolution of the directors authorizing any of the following are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to restore to the company any amounts so distributed or paid and not otherwise recovered by the company and any amounts in relation to any loss suffered by the company:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a redemption or purchase of shares contrary to section 75;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reduction of capital contrary to section 79;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a payment of a dividend contrary to section 83;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a payment of an indemnity contrary to section 221; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any transaction contrary to Part XI.

Section.Text: Subsection 219(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the directors of a company are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to each employee of the company for all debts not exceeding six months wages payable to the employee for services performed for the company while they are directors.

Section.Text: Sections 220 and 221 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A director, officer or employee of a company is not liable under section 216 or 219 or subsection 539(1) and has fulfilled their duty under subsection 166(2) if they exercised the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would have exercised in comparable circumstances, including reliance in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the company that were represented to them by an officer of the company or in a written report of the auditor of the company fairly to reflect the financial condition of the company; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by them.

Subsection.Text: A director or officer of a company has fulfilled their duty under subsection 166(1) if they relied in good faith on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements of the company that were represented to them by an officer of the company or in a written report of the auditor of the company fairly to reflect the financial condition of the company; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report of a person whose profession lends credibility to a statement made by them.

Subsection.Text: A company may indemnify a director or officer of the company, a former director or officer of the company or another person who acts or acted, at the company’s request, as a director or officer of or in a similar capacity for another entity against all costs, charges and expenses, including an amount paid to settle an action or satisfy a judgment, reasonably incurred by them in respect of any civil, criminal, administrative, investigative or other proceeding in which they are involved because of that association with the company or other entity.

A company may advance amounts to the director, officer or other person for the costs, charges and expenses of a proceeding referred to in subsection (1). They shall repay the amounts if they do not fulfil the conditions set out in subsection (3).
A company may not indemnify a person under subsection (1) unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person acted honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of, as the case may be, the company or the other entity for which they acted at the company’s request as a director or officer or in a similar capacity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a criminal or administrative action or proceeding that is enforced by a monetary penalty, the person had reasonable grounds for believing that their conduct was lawful.

Subsection.Text: A company may with the approval of a court indemnify a person referred to in subsection (1) or advance amounts to them under subsection (2) — in respect of an action by or on behalf of the company or other entity to procure a judgment in its favour to which the person is made a party because of the association referred to in subsection (1) with the company or other entity — against all costs, charges and expenses reasonably incurred by them in connection with that action if they fulfil the conditions set out in subsection (3).

Despite subsection (1), a person referred to in that subsection is entitled to be indemnified by the company in respect of all costs, charges and expenses reasonably incurred by them in connection with the defence of any civil, criminal, administrative, investigative or other proceeding to which the person is subject because of the association referred to in subsection (1) with the company or other entity described in that subsection if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was not judged by the court or other competent authority to have committed any fault or omitted to do anything that they ought to have done; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fulfils the conditions set out in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: A company may, to the extent referred to in subsections (1) to (5) in respect of the person, indemnify the heirs or personal representatives of any person whom the company may indemnify under those subsections.

Section.Text: Paragraph 222(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the capacity of a director or officer of another entity or while acting in a similar capacity for another entity, if they act or acted in that capacity at the company’s request, except if the liability relates to a failure to act honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of the entity.

Section.Text: Paragraph 238(1)(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: change the province in which the head office of the company is situated.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 246(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the amalgamated company and the province in which its head office is to be situated;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 246(2.1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the amalgamated society and the province in which its head office is to be situated;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Each share of an applicant carries the right to vote in respect of an amalgamation agreement whether or not it otherwise carries the right to vote.
Subsection 248(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Separate vote for class or series
Subsection.Text: The holders of shares of a class or series of shares of each applicant are entitled to vote separately as a class or series in respect of an amalgamation agreement if the agreement contains a provision that, if it were contained in a proposed amendment to the by-laws or incorporating instrument of the applicant, would entitle those holders to vote separately as a class or series.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 249(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company that does not have any participating policyholders may, without complying with sections 246 to 248, amalgamate with one or more bodies corporate that

Section.Text: Subsections 260(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company shall at all times have a head office in the province specified in its incorporating instrument or by-laws.
The directors of a company may change the address of the head office within the province specified in the incorporating instrument or by-laws.

Section.Text: Subsection 262(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company’s shareholders, policyholders entitled to vote and creditors and their personal representatives may examine the records referred to in subsection 261(1) during the usual business hours of the company and may take extracts from them free of charge or have copies of them made on payment of a reasonable fee. If the company is a distributing company, any other person may on payment of a reasonable fee examine those records during the usual business hours of the company and take extracts from them or have copies of them made.

Section.Text: Subsection 263(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company’s shareholders, policyholders entitled to vote and creditors and their personal representatives are — or if the company is a distributing company, any person is — entitled to a basic list of shareholders of the company.
Subsections 268(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Location and processing of information or data

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), a company shall maintain and process in Canada any information or data relating to the preparation or maintenance of the records referred to in section 261 or of its central securities register unless the Superintendent has, subject to any terms and conditions that the Superintendent considers appropriate, exempted the company from the application of this section.

Subject to subsections (4) and (5), the company may maintain copies of the records referred to in section 261 or of its central securities register outside Canada and may process outside Canada any information or data relating to those copies.
Subsection 268(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le paragraphe (1) ne s’applique pas à l’égard des succursales de la société qui sont situées à l’étranger ou des clients de celles-ci.

Section.Text: Subsection 271(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company’s shareholders, policyholders entitled to vote and creditors and their personal representatives may examine the central securities register during the usual business hours of the company and may take extracts from it free of charge or have copies of it made on payment of a reasonable fee. If the company is a distributing company, any other person may on payment of a reasonable fee examine the central securities register during the usual business hours of the company and take extracts from it or have copies of it made.

The company may make the information contained in the central securities register available by any mechanical or electronic data processing system or other information storage device that is capable of reproducing it in intelligible written form within a reasonable time.

A person who wishes to examine the central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made shall provide the company with an affidavit containing their name and address — or if they are an entity, the name and address for service of the entity — and with an undertaking that the information contained in the register will not be used except in the same way as a list of shareholders may be used under section 265. In the case of an entity, the affidavit is to be sworn by a director or officer of the entity or a person acting in a similar capacity.

A person who wishes to examine a central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the company or its agent to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplementary information to be provided
Subsection.Text: A company or its agent shall provide the supplementary information within

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 10 days after the day on which the central securities register is examined if the changes take place before that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 10 days after the day to which the supplementary information relates if the changes take place on or after the day on which the central securities register is examined.

Section.Text: Section 279 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company may adopt a corporate seal and change one that it adopted.
A document executed on behalf of a company is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.
The definitions distributing company and insider in subsection 288(1) of the Act are repealed.
The definition business combination in subsection 288(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: business combination means an acquisition of all or substantially all of the assets of one body corporate by another, an amalgamation of two or more bodies corporate or any similar reorganization between two or more bodies corporate;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 288(3) and (4) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Sections 289 to 292 of the Act are replaced by the following:
An insider shall submit an insider report in accordance with the regulations.

On application by an insider, the Superintendent may in writing and on any terms that the Superintendent thinks fit exempt the insider from any of the requirements of section 289. The exemption may be given retroactive effect and the Superintendent shall publish the particulars of the exemption and the reasons for it in a periodical available to the public.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes of sections 289 and 290, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: defining “insider” for the purposes of sections 289 and 290;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the form and content of an insider report; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the submission or publication of an insider report.

Section.Text: Sections 293 to 306 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this section, insider means with respect to a distributing company

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of a subsidiary of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of a body corporate that enters into a business combination with the company; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed or retained by the company.

Subsection.Text: No insider may knowingly sell, directly or indirectly, a security of a distributing company or of any of the distributing company’s affiliates if the insider does not own or has not fully paid for the security.

Despite subsection (2), an insider may sell a security that they do not own if they own another security that is convertible into the security that was sold or they own an option or right to acquire the security that was sold, and if within 10 days after the sale they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exercise the conversion privilege, option or right and deliver the security so acquired to the purchaser; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: transfer the convertible security, option or right to the purchaser.

Subsection.Text: No insider may knowingly, directly or indirectly, buy or sell a call or put in respect of a security of a company or of any of the company’s affiliates.
In this section and sections 294.1 and 295, insider with respect to a company means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an affiliate of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a director or officer of the company or of any person described in paragraph (b), (d) or (f);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person who beneficially owns directly or indirectly, or who exercises control or direction over or has a combination of ownership, control and direction in respect of, shares of the company carrying more than the prescribed percentage of the voting rights attached to all of the company’s outstanding shares not including shares held by the person as underwriter while those shares are in the course of a distribution to the public;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a person, other than a person described in paragraph (f), who is employed or retained by the company or by a person described in paragraph (f);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who engages in or proposes to engage in any business or professional activity with or on behalf of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a person who received material confidential information concerning the company while they were a person described in any of paragraphs (a) to (f);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a person who receives material confidential information from a person who is and who they know or ought reasonably to have known is a person described in this subsection, including in this paragraph, or subsection (3) or (4); or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, each of the following is deemed to be a security of a company:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a put, call, option or other right or obligation to purchase or sell a security of the company; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a security of another entity, the market price of which varies materially with the market price of the securities of the company.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed insider — take-over bid or business combination
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and subsection 294.1(1), a person who proposes to make a take-over bid as defined in the regulations for securities of a company or to enter into a business combination with a company is an insider of the company with respect to material confidential information obtained from the company.

An insider of a person referred to in subsection (3), or the person’s affiliate or associate, is an insider of the company referred to in that subsection. Paragraphs (1)(b) to (i) apply in making this determination except that references to “company” are to be read as references to “person described in subsection (3)”.
In subsection (4), associate means with respect to a person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a body corporate that the person directly or indirectly controls, determined without regard to paragraph 3(1)(d), or of which they beneficially own shares or securities currently convertible into shares carrying more than 10% of the voting rights under all circumstances or by reason of the occurrence of an event that has occurred and is continuing or a currently exercisable option or right to purchase the shares or convertible securities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a partner of the person acting on behalf of the partnership of which they are partners;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trust or estate in which the person has a substantial beneficial interest or in respect of which they serve as a trustee or a liquidator of the succession or in a similar capacity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a spouse or common-law partner of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a child of the person or of their spouse or common-law partner; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if that relative has the same residence as the person, a relative of the person or of their spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Insider trading — compensation to sellers and purchasers
Subsection.Text: An insider of a company who purchases or sells a security of the company with knowledge of confidential information that if it were generally known might reasonably be expected to materially affect the value of any of the securities of the company is liable to compensate the seller or purchaser of the security, as the case may be, for any loss suffered by them as a result of the purchase or sale unless the insider establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the insider reasonably believed that the information had been generally disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information was known or ought reasonably to have been known by the seller or purchaser; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase or sale of the security took place in the prescribed circumstances.

Subsection.Text: The insider is accountable to the company for any benefit or advantage received or receivable by the insider as a result of a purchase or sale described in subsection (5), unless they establish the circumstances described in paragraph (6)(a).

Section.MarginalNote: Tipping — compensation to sellers and purchasers

Subsection.Text: An insider of a company who discloses confidential information with respect to the company that has not been generally disclosed and that if it were generally known might reasonably be expected to materially affect the value of any of the securities of the company is liable to compensate any person who subsequently sells securities of the company to or purchases them from any person who received the information unless the insider establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the insider reasonably believed that the information had been generally disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information was known or ought reasonably to have been known by the person who alleges that they suffered the loss;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the insider is not a person described in subsection 294(3) or (4), the disclosure of the information was necessary in the course of their business; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the insider is a person described in subsection 294(3) or (4), the disclosure of the information was necessary to effect the take-over bid or business combination.

Subsection.Text: The insider is accountable to the company for any benefit or advantage received or receivable by them as a result of a disclosure of information as described in subsection (1) unless they establish the circumstances described in paragraph (1)(a), (c) or (d).

The court may assess damages under subsection 294(6) or 294.1(1) in accord­ance with any measure of damages that it considers relevant in the circumstances. However, in assessing damages in respect of a security of a distributing company, the court shall consider the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the plaintiff is a purchaser, the price that they paid for the security less the average market price of the security over the 20 trading days immediately following general disclosure of the information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the plaintiff is a seller, the average market price of the security over the 20 trading days immediately following general disclosure of the information, less the price that they received for the security.

Subsection.Text: If more than one insider is liable under subsection 294(6) or 294.1(1) with respect to the same transaction or series of transactions, their liability is joint and several, or solidary.
An action to enforce a right created by subsection 294(6) or (7) or section 294.1 may be commenced only within two years after discovery of the facts that gave rise to the cause of action.
No person including a company shall distribute securities of a company except in accordance with the regulations made under subsection (2).
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the distribution of securities of a company, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
